titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lucy Kramer Cohen Papers
titlestmt.author: Jennifer Meehan, Jonathan Harmon, and Stephanie Bredbenner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9344905.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday December 17, 2020 at 04:03
Finding aid revised to reflect comprehensive rearrangement and reprocessing.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English, some materials in German and Yiddish.

did.abstract: The collection documents the life and career of Lucy Kramer Cohen, particularly her work for several federal agencies, contributions to The Handbook of Federal Indian Law , and her political and advocacy work. Professional papers document Kramer Cohen's work for the Department of the Interior, Department of Agriculture, National War Labor Board, Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, Department of Labor, National Science Foundation, Public Health Service, Helen Gahagan Douglas, and Human Relations Area Files. The collection also contains writings by Kramer Cohen and her husband Felix S. Cohen and correspondence concerning Lucy Kramer Cohen's editing of her husband's writings and management of his literary estate. Also present are correspondence, reports, clippings, and printed material concerning the Cohens' involvement with various political and social causes. Material relating to Kramer Cohen's art projects includes correspondence and notes documenting her work with Italian artist Pietro Lazzari. Finally, personal and family papers include personal correspondence, photographs, and memorabilia concerning the Cohens, their daughters, and their extended family.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 49, 85-92 (record album storage), Box 102 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Karen Holmes and Gene Tweraser, 2009, 2014, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Professional Papers, 1927-1989. II. Writings, 1931-2005. III. Political and Advocacy Activities, 1920-1995. IV. Art Projects, 1926-2006. V. Personal and Family Papers, 1900-2005. VI. Printed Materials, 1902-1995.

bioghist.head: Lucy Kramer Cohen (1907-2007)
bioghist.p: Lucy Kramer Cohen was born to Aaron Kramer and Annie Frankel Kramer in Brooklyn, New York in 1907. She graduated from Barnard College in 1928 and earned her master's degree in mathematics and anthropology from Columbia University in 1929, where she studied under Franz Boas. She married Felix S. Cohen, a lawyer, in 1931, and they had two daughters, Karen and Gene. Kramer Cohen worked as an unpaid assistant with her husband in the United States Department of the Interior, helping to draft the Indian Reorganization Act of 1934 and The Handbook of Federal Indian Law in 1942. Kramer Cohen also worked for several other federal agencies from the 1930s until her retirement in 1989, including the Department of Agriculture, War Labor Board, Department of Labor, National Science Foundation, and the Public Health Service. Although she officially retired in 1977, she continued to work on annual contracts until 1989.

In addition to her government work, Kramer Cohen served on the staff of Representative Helen Gahagan Douglas (D-Calif.) during Douglas's three terms in the House from 1944 to 1950 and worked on Douglas's campaign for senator in 1950. Between her government jobs, she did research work for anthropologists in Washington, including research and writing for Interior Secretary Harold Ickes. Kramer Cohen also worked for the Food and Agricultural Organization of the United Nations and as an editor of the Human Relations Area Files.

After Felix S. Cohen's death in 1953, Kramer Cohen collected, edited, and published his work as The Legal Conscience (New Haven, Yale University Press: 1960). She also edited her husband's writings about Puerto Rico into a book that was never published, and managed the copyright and reprint rights of his writings after his death.

Kramer Cohen and her husband were involved in many political and social causes and organizations, including Native American rights, the Socialist Party, anti-fascist organizations, and the League for Industrial Democracy. She served on the board of directors of the Association on American Indian Affairs from 1956 until 1994. In her later years, she took up painting and worked to promote the work of Italian artist and sculptor Pietro Lazzari. She died of a stroke in 2007.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection contains several boxes of Felix S. Cohen's papers, particularly in Series II. Writings and Series V. Personal and Family Papers. These papers were kept with Lucy Kramer Cohen's papers to indicate that she retained them after her husband's death and after the donation of the rest of his papers to Yale in 1989 and 1991. She likely used these files for professional or personal projects, most notably in editing her husband's writings.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Present throughout the collection are manuscript notes, often on envelopes and sticky notes, in the hands of Lucy Kramer Cohen, her daughters, and previous processing archivists. Notes often summarize the contents of folders, provide additional context about the people and projects mentioned, and sometimes indicate where in the Cohen house the material was found.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: 1 box of Lucy Kramer Cohen's papers was removed from the Felix S. Cohen papers (WA MSS S-1325) and added to the Lucy Kramer Cohen papers during reprocessing in 2019-2020.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lucy Kramer Cohen Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was put in its current arrangement during comprehensive reprocessing that occurred in 2019-2020. Originally the collection was organized into two groupings corresponding with the first two acquisitions in 2009 and 2014. There were often detailed folder titles but no series arrangement, which was imposed during reprocessing.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Most folder titles have been transcribed directly from Lucy Kramer Cohen's original folders. Devised folder titles and additions to existing folder titles have been enclosed in brackets.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Series VI. Printed Material is not representative of all printed material present in the collection. Some printed materials were left in their original folders to preserve the context of Kramer Cohen's professional and personal activities. In particular, printed material related to her professional work, often authored by Kramer Cohen in the course of her work for various United States government agencies, was usually kept in the originating folders in the professional papers series.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Felix S. Cohen Papers (WA MSS S-1325). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the life and career of Lucy Kramer Cohen, particularly her work for several federal agencies, contributions to The Handbook of Federal Indian Law , and her political and advocacy work. Professional papers primarily consist of correspondence, memoranda, reports, research notes, printed materials, and other papers relating to Kramer Cohen's work for the Department of the Interior, Department of Agriculture, National War Labor Board, Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, Department of Labor, National Science Foundation, and Public Health Service. Other professional papers include speeches and notes from Kramer Cohen's time as a staffer and speechwriter for Helen Gahagan Douglas and memoranda and reports on Korea, Germany, and Iran from her time as an editor of the Human Relations Area Files. Other professional papers relate to Kramer Cohen's work as a high school math teacher, research jobs, job searches, and the federal government's loyalty investigation concerning Kramer Cohen in the 1950s.

The collection also contains writings by Kramer Cohen and her husband Felix S. Cohen, including notes and correspondence concerning the publication of The Legal Conscience , Felix S. Cohen's correspondence concerning Puerto Rico, and correspondence concerning Lucy Kramer Cohen's editing of her husband's writings and management of his literary estate. Also present are correspondence, reports, clippings, and printed material concerning the Cohens' involvement with political and social causes, including board meeting minutes and cassette tapes relating to the Association on American Indian Affairs. Material relating to Kramer Cohen's art projects includes correspondence and notes documenting her work with Italian artist Pietro Lazzari and copies of the newsletter of the Washington Water Color Association, which she edited. Finally, personal and family papers include personal correspondence, photographs, and memorabilia concerning the Cohens, their daughters, and their extended family. Includes audiovisual recordings of Felix S. Cohen's memorial service and legal and financial records concerning the estate of Lucy Kramer Cohen's father Aaron Kramer.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Lucy Kramer Cohen Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

persname.part: Cohen, Felix S., 1907-1953 (Felix S. Cohen's Handbook of federal Indian law)

corpname.part: United States. Department of the Interior
United States. National War Labor Board (1942-1945)
Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, reports, notes, printed material, and other papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's career, particularly her work for the Department of Labor, Helen Gahagan Douglas, and the Public Health Service. Also includes papers concerning her work for the Department of the Interior, Department of Agriculture, National War Labor Board, Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, Human Relations Area File, National Science Foundation, and other work as a high school teacher and freelance researcher and typist. The other professional papers subseries includes records of the federal government's loyalty investigations about Kramer Cohen, 1950-1952.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten subseries: Department of the Interior, Department of Agriculture, National War Labor Board, Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, Department of Labor, Representative Helen Gahagan Douglas, Human Relations Area Files, National Science Foundation, Public Health Service, and Other Professional Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, reports, notes, and printed material documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work for the Department of the Interior, where she prepared surveys for tribes and anthropologists about the Indian Reorganization Act, summarized survey responses, assisted in drafting tribal constitutions, conducted research, and wrote articles for Interior's Indians at Work publication.

did.unittitle: Notes and printed material related to history of Indian law
Timeline of early European exploration and discovery vs. effect on Indians
Papers from collaboration with Felix S. Cohen on Indian affairs
Correspondence and writings related to Indian work

Correspondence and research notes on Indian history, culture, and language
Printed material and research notes on Indians

Notes and drafts of "Red Man's Gifts" chapters 1 and 2

Transcript of radio program by Indian Bureau, "450 Years of White-Indian Relations"

Notes on immigration and refugees for Indian Department
"Indians: Yesterday and Today" with 2 chapters by LKC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, reports, and notes documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work for the Department of Agriculture, particularly her work collecting data about children's clothing sizes and dried skim milk.

did.unittitle: Research materials re: dried skim milk
Vitamin B research dried skim milk
Notes and research materials on stature of 3 and 4 year olds

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, reports, and notes documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work for the National War Labor Board, particularly concerning the West Coast Labor Commission, wages, and the lumber, steel, salmon, and textile industries.

did.unittitle: Reports, memoranda and printed material related to the West Coast Labor Commission, veteran's rights and the National Conciliation Service

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, notes, and reports documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work for the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, particularly her work concerning poultry and egg statistics and rural welfare.

did.unittitle: Charts of eggs and poultry statistics
Poultry and egg report press release and Wall Street Journal article
Eggs and poultry livestock products review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, reports, notes, clippings, and printed material documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work for the Department of Labor, particularly concerning union contracts, collective bargaining, wage stabilization, and the Mexican Farm Labor Program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports, research notes, clippings, and drafts documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work as a researcher and campaign staffer for Congresswoman Helen Gahagan Douglas, particularly concerning research about cost of living in California and Douglas's 1950 Senate campaign.

did.unittitle: Helen Gahagan Douglas writings and clippings
Helen Gahagan Douglas cost of living speech and related material
Research notes for Helen Gahagan Douglas
Survey of Current Business, profiles of the incomes of physicians, dentists, and lawyers
Helen Gahagan Douglas campaign material, signed photograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memoranda, newsletters, research notes, and reports documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work as editor of the Washington Human Relations Area File, including reports on the Federal Republic of Germany, the Republic of Kora, and Iran.

did.unittitle: Human Relations Area Files newsletters and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials, notes, press releases, and printed material documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work for the National Science Foundation's President's Commission on Scientists and Engineers.

did.unittitle: Research materials and scientific and technical labor force
The President's Committee on Scientists and Engineers, printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, reports, research notes, newsletters, pamphlets, printed material, and other papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work for the United States Public Health Service, particularly concerning women physicians and health labor shortages. Also includes personnel documents concerning Kramer Cohen's employment, pay, promotions, and retirement.

did.unittitle: Employment documents and "Scientific and Technological Manpower News Round-up"
Department of Health, Education and Welfare, personnel papers
Department of Health, Education and Welfare, personnel papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, and notes concerning other aspects of Lucy Kramer Cohen's career, including her work as a high school mathematics teacher, freelance research and typing jobs, job searches, retirement, and the federal government's loyalty investigation about her.

did.unittitle: Social Science Research Council, "A Survey of Research in the Field of Industrial Relations"
Social Science Research Council, "A Survey of Research in the Field of Industrial Relations"
Notes and correspondence related to the translation for the Encyclopedia ofSocial Services
Correspondence regarding Lucy Kramer Cohen's employment qualifications
"Coverage of Domestic Servants in Foreign Insurance Plans"

Research and correspondence for loyalty investigation
Research and correspondence for loyalty investigation
Forms and correspondence related to loyalty investigation
National Bureau of Economic Research, Inc. memoranda and correspondence
Civil service correspondence and employment application
Papers related to the American Anthropological Association meeting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, outlines, and printed material concerning Lucy Kramer Cohen's writing projects, primarily her editing of her husband Felix S. Cohen's writings after his death in 1953. Includes correspondence, notes, and printed material concerning The Legal Conscience , a collection of Felix S. Cohen's writings edited by Lucy Kramer Cohen and published in 1960, as well as correspondence and an outline for a proposed book, never published, compiling Felix S. Cohen's writings about Puerto Rico. Includes portions of Felix S. Cohen's papers, primarily correspondence and memoranda concerning Puerto Rico. The Other Writing Projects subseries primarily contains correspondence concerning reprints and new editions of Felix S. Cohen's writings after his death.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: The Legal Conscience, Puerto Rico Book Project, and Other Writing Projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, and printed material documenting the editing and publication of The Legal Conscience , published by Yale University Press in 1960. Includes printed materials materials and notes Kramer Cohen used in compiling and editing her husband's writings, correspondence concerning the publication, and reviews of the book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers concerning Lucy Kramer Cohen's efforts to edit her husband's writings about Puerto Rico into a book. Includes Felix S. Cohen's correspondence, memoranda, notes, and printed material about Puerto Rico, 1942-1950, which Kramer Cohen retained after his death in 1953, as well as Kramer Cohen's notes, correspondence with publishers, contracts, and a proposed outline for the book, 1962-1965.

did.unittitle: Felix S. Cohen correspondence, legal writings, and clippings related to Puerto Rican self-governance
Felix S. Cohen correspondence and memoranda relating to Puerto Rico
Felix S. Cohen correspondence and printed material related to Puerto Rico and the Puerto Rico Industrial Development Company
Felix S. Cohen legal papers and political reports on Puerto Rico development plan
"The Use and Disposition of Ships and Shipyards at the End of World War II"
[Felix S. Cohen notes, correspondence, and printed material concerning natural resources in Puerto Rico]
Felix S. Cohen correspondence and printed material related to Puerto Rico
Felix S. Cohen correspondence re: Puerto Rico
Felix S. Cohen correspondence and memoranda related to Puerto Rico tax exemption
Felix S. Cohen correspondence and clippings re: Puerto Rico
"Congressional Intent and Attitude Toward Public Law 600 and the Constitution of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico"
[Correspondence and contracts for book on Puerto Rico]
[Correspondence, contracts, and outline for book on Puerto Rico]
Clipping and notes about Puerto Rico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers concerning other writing projects, including Lucy Kramer Cohen's management of Felix S. Cohen's writings after his death, 1932-2005. Includes a draft of a 1932 anthropology manuscript entitled "Machines and Tools" by Lucy Kramer Cohen, correspondence concerning reprints and new editions of Ethical Systems and Legal Ideals, Handbook of Federal Indian Law , and other writings by Felix S. Cohen, and Kramer Cohen's efforts to compile writings in memory of Felix S. Cohen in a memorial booklet for City College of New York. Includes some photocopies of clippings and Felix S. Cohen's papers used in writing projects by Lucy Kramer Cohen and others.

did.unittitle: [Correspondence and drafts concerning off-prints of Felix S. Cohen article "Reconstruction of Hidden Value Judgments"]
[Materials, clippings, and photocopies used for Felix S. Cohen biography]
[Correspondence concerning Felix S. Cohen writings and CCNY memorial booklet]
[University of New Mexico correspondence, printed materials, and banquet concerning 1971 edition of Handbook of Federal Indian Law]
[Ethical Systems and Legal Ideals publishing rights correspondence]
[Handbook of Federal Indian Law revision correspondence]
[Correspondence and acknowledgments of 1983 edition of Handbook of Federal Indian Law]
[New Handbook edition and Indian Law at a Crossroads conference]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen and Felix S. Cohen's political and advocacy work, 1920-1995. Documents the Cohens' involvement with the Socialist Party, anti-fascist organizations during World War II, the Capital City Forum, and the League for Industrial Democracy. Also includes papers documenting Kramer Cohen's advocacy work after her husband's death in 1953, including her service on the board of the Association for American Indian Affairs and her involvement in the opening of the Felix S. Cohen Memorial Home, a home for the elderly on Pine Ridge Indian Reservation named in honor of her husband. Material primarily consists of correspondence, reports, notes, and printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Alphabetical Files, League for Industrial Democracy, Capital City Forum, Association on American Indian Affairs, Felix S. Cohen Memorial Home, and Other Political and Advocacy Work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen and Felix S. Cohen's political and advocacy activities, 1936-1948, which Kramer Cohen arranged and maintained in alphabetical file folders. Includes correspondence, memoranda, and printed material concerning the Socialist Party, the National Committee for the Revision of the Comstock Law, the League for Industrial Democracy, and various anti-interventionist and anti-fascist organizations during World War II, including "Inside Germany," anti-Nazi reports published by the Friends of German Freedom, and several issues of the anti-interventionist newsletter Uncensored, published by the Writers Anti-War Bureau. Includes some personal correspondence and bills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers, primarily correspondence, documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen and Felix S. Cohen's involvement with the League for Industrial Democracy, a socialist labor organization, 1929-1935. Contains extensive correspondence concerning the Industrial Research Group of the League for Industrial Democracy, on which Lucy Kramer Cohen served. Includes correspondence, notes, reports, drafts, and printed material.

did.unittitle: Industrial Research Group correspondence and reports
Correspondence and writings, Industrial Research Group
Industrial Research Group, Fabian Society correspondence
Industrial Research Group correspondence and notes
"A Survey of Social Problems in the Production and Consumption of Food"
Industrial Research Group manuscripts and reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's involvement with the Capital City Forum, a socialist group that organized lectures and other events in Washington, D.C., 1936-1938. Primarily consists of membership cards and correspondence concerning the group's events, as well as some reports and clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's involvement with the Association on American Indian Affairs, including her service on the board beginning in 1954. Includes correspondence, memoranda, board meeting minutes, and notes. Also includes correspondence, promotional materials, and cassette tapes concerning the association's conference on Indian self-governance held in Sun Valley, Idaho in 1983 to commemorate the fiftieth anniversary of the Indian Reorganization Act.

did.unittitle: Association on American Indian Affairs clippings and memoranda
Indian self-rule conference correspondence and notes
Indian self-rule conference correspondence and notes
Indian self-rule conference correspondence, publicity material, and photographs
Interview with Lucy Kramer Cohen at the Sun Valley, Idaho conference "50 Years of the Indian Reorganization Act"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lucy Kramer Cohen interviewed by Marjorie Ambler

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Panel at Indian self-rule conference, "John Collier, Felix Cohen, and the Adoption of the IRA" 1 of 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Panel featured Lucy Kramer Cohen, C. Walkup, and B. Reifel

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Documents supporting Michael Trujillo's nomination for Indian Health Service directorship

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence concerning Lucy Kramer Cohen's involvement in the planning, fundraising, and dedication of the Felix S. Cohen Memorial Home, a home for the elderly on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation in South Dakota, 1962-1964.

did.unittitle: Felix S. Cohen Memorial Home correspondence and dedication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen and Felix S. Cohen's involvement with other political and social causes, including the Washington Consumers Group, the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People, the Lawyers' Committee on American Relations with Spain, the Institute of Living Law, and the American Indian Policy Review Commission, 1920-1976. Primarily consists of correspondence, memoranda, notes, and printed material, including several folders of clippings about the Spanish Civil War. Some of the material in this subseries may overlap with organizations in previous Political and Advocacy Work subseries, such as the League for Industrial Democracy, the Capital City Forum, and the Socialist Party.

did.unittitle: Writings, correspondence, and printed material, Student Summer Forum
Invitations for Washington Committee for National Sharecroppers
Correspondence and bulletins related to the National Emergency Council Consumer Division
Flyers and member literature for the Washington Consumers's Group and co-op
Clippings related to the Spanish Civil War and the Washington League of Women Shoppers
Writings and correspondence NAACP and lynching
Lawyers' Committee on American Relations with Spain
Lawyers' Committee on American Relations with Spain
[Affidavits concerning the loyalty investigation of Val R. Lorwin]
American Indian Policy Review Commission correspondence and memoranda
Manuscript of address radio broadcast, Institute of Living Law

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, promotional materials, and photographs documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's work as an artist, particularly her work collaborating with and promoting the work of Italian artist Pietro Lazzari. Also includes material related to Kramer Cohen's own art shows and her involvement with the Washington Water Color Association.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Pietro Lazzari and Washington Water Color Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, promotional materials, and photographs documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's collaborations with Pietro Lazzari. Kramer Cohen wrote correspondence for Lazzari and publicized his projects and shows, including his busts of Eleanor Roosevelt and Norman Thomas. Lucy Kramer was also involved in writing Lazzari's obituary after his death in 1979.

did.unittitle: Lazzari clippings and photographs related to publicity
Lazzari notes, clippings, and publicity materials
Eleanor Roosevelt bust correspondence and clippings
Correspondence, clippings, notes, and speech relating to Eleanor Roosevelt bust
Lazzari early shows clippings and correspondence
Eleanor Roosevelt bust correspondence and clippings
Lazzari manuscripts, description of a dream
Eleanor Roosevelt correspondence, clippings, and notes
Norman Thomas and Eleanor Roosevelt correspondence
Solicitation of bust of Eleanor Roosevelt
Eleanor Roosevelt bust correspondence and photograph
Bust of Pope Paul VI correspondence
Photographs, acceptance of prize for bust
Response to desposition of Margaret Herring
Lazzari notes, clippings, photographs, and obituary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, drafts, newsletters, and promotional materials documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's involvement with the Washington Water Color Association, including the association's art shows and newsletter, which Kramer Cohen edited for several years.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers documenting the personal and family lives of Lucy Kramer Cohen, her husband Felix S. Cohen, and her father Aaron Kramer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Personal Papers and Family Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers documenting Lucy Kramer Cohen's personal life, including her studies, volunteer work, social life, and family life. Primarily contains personal and family correspondence, photographs, notes, address books, and other papers. Also includes several audio recordings of the funeral of her husband Felix S. Cohen in 1953.

did.unittitle: Columbia notes, cards, and book list

[Lucy Kramer Cohen and Felix S. Cohen diplomas and prize certificates]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers concerning the Kramer and Cohen families, particularly Felix S. Cohen and Aaron Kramer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Felix S. Cohen Papers and Aaron Kramer Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers, including personal and professional correspondence and photographs, concerning Felix S. Cohen. Papers document Cohen's family life, undergraduate and graduate studies, and legal career. Also includes photographs, medals and awards, and legal certificates.

did.unittitle: Department of the Interior distinguished service award
[Undated Felix S. Cohen correspondence and notes]
Correspondence with Mr. Ironman, paper entitled "Law and Language"
[Photocopies concerning Felix S. Cohen's New Mexico voting rights case]
Letter regarding Nat Holman to The Campus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and legal records concerning Lucy Kramer Cohen's father Aaron Kramer, including his estate after his death in 1974.

did.unittitle: [Aaron Kramer papers, legal records, and correspondence]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials collected by Lucy Kramer Cohen and Felix S. Cohen. Includes articles about anthropology, science, literature, art, law, and Native Americans.

did.unittitle: [Federal agriculture programs and union printed material]
[Industrial Ladies' Garment Workers' Union printed material]
"The New Orleans Meeting of the American Association for the Advancement of Science"
[Lucy Kramer Cohen and Felix S. Cohen publications]
Annotated "The Logic of Semantics," Journal of Philosophy
[Printed material by and in honor of Felix S. Cohen]

Annotated Oliver La Farge and the American Indian
"'A Year and A Spring of My Existence': Felix S. Cohen and the Handbook of Federal Indian Law"
